baseline.notes: Run 43 baseline: F1 0.409. Run 48 (yolo_crop) regressed Rush to 0.360 (-0.049) because tightening to the player cluster stripped spatial context the prompt relies on for type discrimination (goal_kick -3 TP, throw_in -3 TP). Run 49 replaces that with field-detection crop: per-video HSV green segmentation finds the pitch region, crops out only non-pitch content (sky, parking, crowd). Smoke test on Rush frames showed bbox y=[0.24,1.0] full width -> only the top 24% (trees above far touchline) is removed, pitch content fully preserved.

targets.notes: Rush F1 MUST hold >=0.409. The whole premise of field-detection crop is that removing only non-pitch regions preserves the spatial context the prompt needs. If Rush still regresses, the hypothesis about spatial context being the issue was wrong and we need a different angle entirely. If Rush holds or improves, immediately queue Run 50 on sporting_ac.

field_crop_enabled.reason: Per-video HSV field detection. Rush bbox: y=[0.236, 1.000], full x — removes only top 24% (trees above the field on far touchline).

yolo_crop_enabled.reason: Disabled — field_crop replaces it with the same intent but preserves spatial context.

notes: Run #49 (commit 12aa0fd): field-detection crop on Rush. Worker restarted at 07:07:00Z, bbox smoke-tested visually on 3 sporting_ac + 3 Rush frames before submission. Expected: near-neutral effect on Rush since its camera already fills the frame with field. Pre-flight pending.
